---
title: "The two-tier vocabulary"
subtitle: "How cybedtools represents heterogeneous frameworks in a single graph"
---

## The design problem

Eight frameworks. Each one was authored against a different theory of
what counts as a structural element. NICE has work roles, tasks,
knowledge statements, and skill statements. DigComp has competence
areas and proficiency levels. SFIA has skills and seven levels of
responsibility. Cyber.org K-12 has grade-band-by-sub-concept cells
plus prose clarification statements. CSTA has level-by-concept cells
plus practices. CSEC2017 has eight knowledge areas. ECSF has twelve
profiles. DCWF aligns to NICE work roles with overlap.

A single shared vocabulary that flattens these into one type hierarchy
would erase the differences each framework was designed to express. A
pure per-framework vocabulary with no shared terms would make
cross-framework queries impossible. The schema has to preserve native
vocabulary and enable shared queries at the same time.

## The two-tier solution

::: {.column-margin}
**A graph in 30 seconds.** A graph is nodes connected by labeled
edges. A query asks "find every node where these edges hold." That
is enough to read the diagram below. The technical names beneath
each box show what the same graph looks like as RDF, but you do not
need to know RDF or SPARQL to follow the structure.
:::

::: {.column-page}

```{=html}
<figure class="schema-diagram">
<button type="button" class="toggle-all" aria-pressed="false" aria-label="Toggle schema names for all elements"><span class="toggle-icon" aria-hidden="true">▸</span><span class="toggle-text">schema names</span></button>
<svg viewBox="0 0 720 540" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-labelledby="schema-title schema-desc">
  <title id="schema-title">cybedtools two-tier vocabulary architecture</title>
  <desc id="schema-desc">A nested-rectangle diagram showing how a Framework relates to its top-level Organizing units (which contain Roles as a subclass for workforce frameworks), and how Organizing units relate to Elements (containing Sub-elements as a subclass) and Examples (a separate predicate).</desc>
  <defs><marker id="schema-arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8.5" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse"><path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.55"/></marker></defs>
  <g stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.4" fill="none" opacity="0.55">
    <line x1="360" y1="72" x2="360" y2="118" marker-end="url(#schema-arrow)"/>
    <line x1="280" y1="284" x2="218" y2="350" marker-end="url(#schema-arrow)"/>
    <line x1="450" y1="284" x2="540" y2="362" marker-end="url(#schema-arrow)" stroke-dasharray="5,4"/>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Framework">
    <rect x="270" y="20" width="180" height="50" rx="6" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.10)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6"/>
    <text class="lp" x="360" y="51" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Framework</text>
    <text class="lt" x="360" y="51" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cybed:Framework</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Organizing unit">
    <rect x="100" y="120" width="520" height="160" rx="8" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.07)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6"/>
    <text class="lp" x="360" y="146" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Organizing unit</text>
    <text class="lt" x="360" y="146" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cybed:OrganizingUnit</text>
    <text class="lp" x="360" y="164" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">every framework's top-level units assert this</text>
    <text class="lt" x="360" y="164" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">every framework asserts this on its top-level units</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Role">
    <rect x="200" y="184" width="320" height="80" rx="6" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.16)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6"/>
    <text class="lp" x="360" y="216" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Role</text>
    <text class="lt" x="360" y="216" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cybed:Role</text>
    <text class="lp" x="360" y="234" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">a kind of organizing unit · workforce frameworks only</text>
    <text class="lt" x="360" y="234" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">subClassOf · workforce frameworks only</text>
    <text class="lp" x="360" y="250" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">(NICE, DCWF, ECSF)</text>
    <text class="lt" x="360" y="250" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">(NICE, DCWF, ECSF)</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Element">
    <rect x="40" y="350" width="340" height="170" rx="8" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.07)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6"/>
    <text class="lp" x="210" y="378" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Element</text>
    <text class="lt" x="210" y="378" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cybed:RoleElement</text>
    <text class="lp" x="210" y="396" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">structural pieces attached to an organizing unit</text>
    <text class="lt" x="210" y="396" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">attached via cybed:hasElement</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Sub-element">
    <rect x="80" y="420" width="260" height="80" rx="6" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.16)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6"/>
    <text class="lp" x="210" y="452" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Sub-element</text>
    <text class="lt" x="210" y="452" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cybed:Subpoint</text>
    <text class="lp" x="210" y="470" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">a kind of element · points back to its parent</text>
    <text class="lt" x="210" y="470" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">subClassOf · cybed:elaborates parent</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-node" aria-label="Example">
    <rect x="430" y="362" width="240" height="100" rx="8" fill="rgba(14,165,233,0.07)" stroke="#0284C7" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/>
    <text class="lp" x="550" y="397" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">Example</text>
    <text class="lt" x="550" y="397" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="currentColor">cybed:Example</text>
    <text class="lp" x="550" y="415" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">pedagogical scaffolding</text>
    <text class="lt" x="550" y="415" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">pedagogical scaffolding</text>
    <text class="lp" x="550" y="431" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">reached through its own connection,</text>
    <text class="lt" x="550" y="431" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">separate predicate · excluded from</text>
    <text class="lp" x="550" y="447" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">not the default element list</text>
    <text class="lt" x="550" y="447" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">default cybed:hasElement traversals</text>
  </g>

  <g class="cyb-edge-label" aria-label="Framework-to-organizing-unit connection">
    <text class="ep" x="368" y="100" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">contains</text>
    <text class="et" x="368" y="100" font-family="JetBrains Mono,monospace" font-size="10.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">cybed:hasOrganizingUnit</text>
  </g>
  <g class="cyb-edge-label" aria-label="Organizing-unit-to-element connection">
    <text class="ep" x="240" y="322" text-anchor="end" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">has element</text>
    <text class="et" x="240" y="322" text-anchor="end" font-family="JetBrains Mono,monospace" font-size="10.5" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">cybed:hasElement</text>
  </g>
  <g class="cyb-edge-label" aria-label="Organizing-unit-to-example connection">
    <text class="ep" x="498" y="322" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11.5" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">has example</text>
    <text class="et" x="498" y="322" font-family="JetBrains Mono,monospace" font-size="10.5" font-style="italic" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.7">cybed:hasExample</text>
  </g>
</svg>
<figcaption>The two-tier shape. Outer rectangles are cross-framework abstracts. Nested rectangles are subclasses. The dashed border on the <em>example</em> box marks it as reachable through a separate connection, not the default element list. Click any box or edge label to flip between plain English and the technical schema name.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

:::

`cybedtools` uses a two-tier RDF vocabulary.

### Tier 1: `cybed:` cross-framework abstract terms

Every framework asserts these. They define the joinable surface for cross-framework queries.

### Tier 2: per-framework subtypes

Each framework defines its own vocabulary that subclasses the `cybed:` abstracts. These preserve the framework's native terminology.

The two-tier shape lets a researcher write one SPARQL query that runs
across the entire corpus at the Tier 1 surface, without losing the
ability to ask framework-specific questions through Tier 2 subtypes.

## Tier 1 vocabulary

| Term | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cybed:Framework` | Class | The framework as a whole |
| `cybed:OrganizingUnit` | Class (subClassOf `skos:Concept`) | Any top-level enumerated unit. All frameworks in the corpus assert this on their parents. |
| `cybed:Role` | Class (subClassOf `cybed:OrganizingUnit`) | Workforce-restricted. NICE work roles, DCWF work roles, ECSF profiles only. |
| `cybed:RoleElement` | Class | Anything attached to an organizing unit via `cybed:hasElement` |
| `cybed:Subpoint` | Class (subClassOf `cybed:RoleElement`) | Bullet points and enumerations embedded inside an element's text |
| `cybed:Example` | Class (subClassOf `cybed:RoleElement`) | Pedagogical scaffolding (such as clarification statements). Reachable only via `cybed:hasExample` |
| `cybed:hasOrganizingUnit` | Property | Framework → organizing unit |
| `cybed:hasRole` | Property | Framework → role (workforce subset) |
| `cybed:hasElement` | Property | Organizing unit → RoleElement (excluding Examples by default) |
| `cybed:hasExample` | Property | Organizing unit → Example (separate predicate) |
| `cybed:elaborates` | Property | Subpoint → parent (back-edge) |

## Tier 2 vocabulary (per-framework subtypes)

Each framework declares its own per-framework namespace and subclasses
the Tier 1 abstracts:

| Framework | Namespace | Per-framework subtypes |
|---|---|---|
| NICE | `nice:` | `nice:Framework`, `nice:WorkRole`, `nice:Task`, `nice:KnowledgeStatement`, `nice:SkillStatement` |
| DCWF | `dcwf:` | `dcwf:Framework`, `dcwf:WorkRole`, `dcwf:Task`, `dcwf:KSA` |
| ECSF | `ecsf:` | `ecsf:Framework`, `ecsf:Profile`, `ecsf:KSA` |
| SFIA | `sfia:` | `sfia:Framework`, `sfia:Skill`, `sfia:LevelDescriptor` |
| Cyber.org K-12 | `cyberorg:` | `cyberorg:Framework`, `cyberorg:StandardGroup`, `cyberorg:Standard` |
| CSTA | `csta:` | `csta:Framework`, `csta:StandardGroup`, `csta:Standard` |
| CSEC2017 | `csec:` | `csec:Framework`, `csec:KnowledgeArea`, `csec:Topic` |
| DigComp | `digcomp:` | `digcomp:Framework`, `digcomp:CompetenceArea`, `digcomp:Competence`, `digcomp:ProficiencyDescriptor` |

A `nice:WorkRole` is also a `cybed:Role`, which is also a
`cybed:OrganizingUnit`, which is also a `skos:Concept`. The chain runs
all the way up the abstraction hierarchy. A query at any tier returns
results from every framework that asserts the relevant Tier 1 type.

## Why a custom URI

The package uses `https://w3id.org/cybed/ontology#` as the namespace
for `cybed:` terms. The W3ID redirect resolves to the schema
documentation hosted with the package. That gives the vocabulary a
stable, persistent URI that does not depend on any one repository's
URL.

## What's reachable from where

A `cybed:hasElement` traversal on any organizing unit returns
`cybed:Subpoint` nodes, because they subclass `cybed:RoleElement`. It
does not return `cybed:Example` nodes, because the Example predicate
is separate. This is intentional. Examples are pedagogical
scaffolding. Including them in default element traversals would
double-count the same conceptual content under two different shapes.

A query that wants Examples explicitly uses `cybed:hasExample`
instead. The package's `example_framework_bindings()` helper does
exactly this.

## What this enables

The `cybed:OrganizingUnit` abstraction lets a single SPARQL query
return organizing units across the entire corpus. The `cybed:Role`
abstraction restricts that query to the three workforce frameworks
(NICE, DCWF, ECSF). The `cybed:hasElement` predicate returns the
strict numbered-element traversal across every framework that uses
it. The `cybed:hasExample` predicate exposes the pedagogical
scaffolding layer separately, where it exists at all (currently
Cyber.org K-12 and CSTA only).

The two-tier shape is what makes the [density spread
query](../queries/density-spread.qmd) work across every framework
with a single expression rather than reapplying per framework.
